{{Quote|We've adopted a code name for these starships: Penumbra Omen. Avoid them at all costs, my friends. The leader of the so-called Penumbra Omen only briefly identified itself as the "OT-Violator". Their starships seem to be like that of a Hutt smuggler's starcraft, although I certainly doubt any Hutt would be flying any of those ships, at least not like that. Their personality almost seemed... mechanical.|[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Silverline]]|<ref name="SWG:AER" />}}
'''Penumbra Omen''' was the code-name of a mysterious and hostile force of [[Starship/Legends|starships]] present in the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo system]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="SWG:PO" /> Around the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], an unit of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] commanded by [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Silverline]], [[Imperial Authoritarian]], was ambushed in the [[Area D-512]] of the Naboo system and suffered heavy casualties. Captain Silverline reported that these assailants, probably [[Droid/Legends|droids]], piloted starships similar to those used by [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] and indicated that their leader identified itself as the [[OT-Violator]].<ref name="SWG:AER" /> The Empire then considered the so-called Penumbra Omen as incredibly dangerous and prohibited all travel in the Area D-512, renamed Penumbra Omen Quadrant.<ref name="SWG:PO" /> Captain Silverline's emergency report was later intercepted by [[Fghai'g Windrunner]], a pilot of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] who considered to explore the Penumbra Omen quadrant.<ref name="SWG:APR">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=item|A Patrol Report}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Penumbra Omen was mentioned in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]'', a [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|massively multiplayer online roleplaying game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]],<ref name="SWG:PO" /> prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref> Penumbra Omen starfighters were cut from the expansion pack [[Star Wars Galaxies: Jump to Lightspeed|''Jump to Lightspeed'']] on [[October 27]], [[2004]].<ref name="TRE">{{SWGcite|exp=JtL|type=other|directory tree files and source data}}</ref>
